methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Asterra is a Series A–stage space and aerospace company that has raised $20M+ to date. The valuation universe includes 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers spanning the sector, though a narrow peer set of same-stage or adjacent-stage companies with sufficient data density is not available.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable method here. Using total raised ($20M) as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, we derive an implied valuation of $40M. This represents the primary quantitative anchor for the range.

Given limited peer comparability and absence of target revenue disclosure, public-market multiples (median P/S of 25.8x across the five public comps) cannot be reliably applied. The triangulated range of $34M–$46M is therefore supported by the single applicable method, with $40M as the median. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrowly comparable private transactions, undisclosed revenue metrics that would enable public-comp anchoring, and the typically wide dispersion in early-stage space-tech valuations. Any refinement would require either revenue visibility or access to recent comparable Series A closings in adjacent subsectors.
